title: RYE canonical tender
description:
The single canonical object every pipeline stage reads and writes. Superset of the processed-quote CSV line schema + today's tender.json + the tender spine (id/version/status/expiry/URL). Stored versioned as the `payload` JSONB column in the Retool `tenders` table.

Naming convention (deliberate, do not 'fix'): tender/meta fields are snake_case (client_name, day_split, expires_at) to match the spec and Postgres columns; fields INSIDE a line keep the exact fixed CSV field names (camelCase: unitRate, standingCharge, ...) so lines round-trip through process_quote.py and build_dashboard.py with zero renaming.

id.description: Stable tender identity. Constant across versions (the same tender re-saved keeps this id; `version` increments).

version.description: Monotonic version number. Every save writes a new Retool DB row; never overwrite. The register shows the latest per id.

status.description: draft = editable, not yet public; published = live dashboard URL exists; expired = past expires_at, page shows the 'expired — contact RYE' state.

tender_label.description: Human tender label, e.g. 'Electricity tender — July 2026'.

utility.description: Display label only. The engine is fuel-agnostic — rate handling is driven by what's in the lines (single vs day/night, kVA present or not).

created_at.description: RFC 3339 UTC timestamp this version was written.

created_by.description: Team member who saved this version (their Retool/SSO email).

expires_at.description: Link lifecycle date. Defaults to the supplier quote's validity date so the URL guards against clients acting on stale pricing. After this date the page shows the expired state. Null = no expiry set.

day_split.description: Day fraction for two-rate meters (night = 1 - day_split - weekend_split). Always disclosed as a dashboard footnote. Set from a known profile / half-hourly data where available.

weekend_split.description: Fraction of annual consumption at the weekend rate, for suppliers pricing weekends as a distinct band. Default 0 = weekend rate captured/shown but not separately costed (a warning is raised). Set this (with day_split) only from a known profile / half-hourly data; night gets the residual 1 - day_split - weekend_split.

url_uuid.description: The unguessable secret in the public URL (rye.energy/<slug>/<url_uuid>). Regenerating this instantly revokes a leaked link. Distinct from `id` so the link can be rotated without changing tender identity.

dashboard_url.description: Full published URL, set by /render once the dashboard is live. Null until published.

charge_basis.description: Tender-wide default charge bases. Overridden per quote by that quote's own charge_basis.

sites.description: The site universe for this tender — the authoritative meter facts (consumption, capacity, our name), keyed by mpxn. EAC and kVA live HERE, once, not on every quote line: they are properties of the meter, not of a supplier's offer, and the comparison must use one consumption basis across all offers. Sourced from RYE's DB / company Postgres where available; from the quote as a fallback.

incumbent.description: The client's current contract, used as the savings baseline. Null when unknown.

quotes.description: One entry per supplier per term. supplier + term is the client-facing offer label.

notes.description: Tender-specific footnotes shown to the client (e.g. 'Drax offer valid until 10 July').

mpxn.description: MPAN (electricity) or MPRN (gas) — the meter point. The universal join key between RYE's data, the site reference DB, and every supplier line. Always a string (an identifier, never arithmetic); no trailing '.0'.

value.description: A rate or charge, already parsed to a number by the shared deterministic parser (currency/unit decoration stripped, numeric value preserved exactly). null = not quoted / not applicable. Units are NOT converted here — the annualisation basis is declared separately in charge_basis.

site_name.description: RYE's own site label (from our DB via the mpxn join). Falls back to the supplier's address only when the mpxn isn't in our DB.

eac.description: Estimated annual consumption (EAC), or AQ for gas, in kWh. Authoritative consumption basis for the whole comparison.

kva.description: Agreed kVA capacity (HH/capacity sites). null where not applicable.

eac_source.description: Provenance of the EAC/kVA figures for auditability. 'db' = RYE reference data (preferred); 'quote' = taken from the supplier file; 'manual' = operator-entered.

line.description: One supplier's offered rates for one meter point. Rate mode is exclusive: a single-rate line has unitRate set with dayRate/nightRate null; a two-rate line has dayRate/nightRate set with unitRate null. This exclusivity is enforced by the deterministic extractor, never by hand.

supplyStartDate.description: Contract start date for this line, ISO 'YYYY-MM-DD' where present.

unitRate.description: Single (standard) rate, p/kWh. Null on two-rate lines.

dayRate.description: Day rate, p/kWh. Null on single-rate lines.

nightRate.description: Night rate, p/kWh. Null on single-rate lines.

weekendRate.description: Weekend rate, p/kWh, where a supplier prices weekends as a distinct band. Null when not applicable. Captured verbatim; only costed when a weekend_split is set, else surfaced as a warning (see build_dashboard).

charge_basis.description: How each charge is annualised. Only non-default entries need be present. A wrong basis silently mis-ranks offers — the highest-risk failure mode — so every non-default basis is auto-footnoted on the dashboard.

category.description: Drives the offer-type filter. 'fixed' or 'coterminous', or any custom label. Inferred from the term text when omitted.

featured.description: True if this offer is shown on the client dashboard. Every extracted offer is stored on the tender for the record; the dashboard renders only featured ones (up to 2, to avoid overloading the client). Set at the assemble step from the price ranking; the cheapest featured full-coverage offer is the recommendation. When NO offer is flagged (older tenders) the dashboard falls back to showing all.

recommended.description: The offer the dashboard leads with — the takeaway. Defaults to the cheapest full-coverage offer; set explicitly when the call isn't purely on price.

rye_fee.description: RYE's flat SaaS fee (no commission). Lets the client see the net saving. List price £90/site/month unless overridden.

per_site_month.description: Overrides the discount calc if given.

extractResult.description: Output of ONE /extract run (one supplier file). Not a full tender — /assemble concatenates the quotes from several extract runs, merges the sites (deduped on mpxn), attaches the incumbent + meta, and stamps id/version/status to form a valid tender document. Kept as a distinct shape so the extractor stays single-file / single-supplier and headless-testable.
